Sebastien with this patch I noticed we have two base64url functions almost identical. Should we move to one fro both GSS and fscrypt

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Sebastien with this patch I noticed we have two base64url functions almost identical. Should we move to one fro both GSS and fscrypt

That should be feasible. llcrypt_base64url_encode() and gss_base64url_encode() does not use exactly the same interface, but there could be a common function to carry out the encoding itself.

commit.subject: LU-18810 obd: add a parameter to enable filename encoding
commit.message:
LU-18810 obd: add a parameter to enable filename encoding

This patch adds a parameter "enable_fname_encoding" to encode
filename in the logs, uses 64-bit FNV-1a to hash filename and
uses function gss_base64url_encode() to encode the hashed
filename to the printable characters.
The following functions and macros are defined for different
pattens:
- encode_fn_len(fname, len)
- encode_fn(fname)
- encode_fn_dname(len, fname)
- encode_fn_luname(lname)
- encode_fn_opdata(op)
- encode_fn_oied(ent)
- encode_fn_qstr(qstr)
- encode_fn_dentry(dentry)
- encode_fn_file(file)

This is a port of
